site.title: ZeroPress Docs Starter
site.description: A compact documentation starter for writing clear technical pages with ZeroPress.

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.content:
## Install

Create your first documentation page and keep content in preview-data, generated Markdown sources, or your own authoring pipeline.

```bash
npm create zeropress-theme -- --template docs --theme-slug docs-starter
npm install
npm run build
```

## Build

Run `zeropress-build` with this theme and preview-data fixture to produce static HTML.

> [!TIP]
> Keep your source content small at first. Add menus, collections, and custom HTML only after the first build is working.

## Publish

The generated output is static HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and public assets. It can be served by any static host.
pages.excerpt: Create and build a small ZeroPress documentation site.

pages.title: Markdown Compatibility
pages.content:
## Overview

This page shows the Markdown features a documentation theme should make readable. The page title is rendered by the template, so the body begins at `h2`.

## Tables

| Feature | Rendered by build-core | Theme responsibility |
|---|---:|---|
| Heading `id` | Yes | Style scroll targets |
| TOC data | Yes | Place and style navigation |
| Alerts | Yes | Style `zp-alert` blocks |
| Mermaid fences | Preserved as code | Optional progressive enhancement |

## Task Lists

- [x] Render disabled checkboxes
- [x] Keep list text aligned
- [ ] Decide whether to add interactive project tracking elsewhere

## Alerts

> [!NOTE]
> Notes are useful for extra context that should not interrupt the main flow.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> Important callouts should be visually distinct, but still readable in dark mode.

> [!WARNING]
> Warning blocks are content, not UI chrome. They need spacing that works inside prose.

## Code Blocks

```js
export function createPage(title) {
  return {
    title,
    status: 'published',
  };
}
```

## Mermaid Fences

ZeroPress preserves Mermaid as a fenced code block. A theme or site can add client-side rendering later.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  Markdown --> BuildCore
  BuildCore --> HTML
  HTML --> Theme
```

## Inline Elements

Use `inline code`, **strong emphasis**, _emphasis_, ~~strikethrough~~, and keyboard hints like <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>K</kbd> when they help the reader scan.

## Nested Structure

### A third-level section

Use h3 for local grouping.

#### A fourth-level section

Use h4 sparingly. It appears in the TOC, so it should represent a useful navigation target.

## Blockquotes

> Documentation should be calm enough to read twice and structured enough to scan once.
pages.excerpt: A compact page that exercises tables, task lists, alerts, code fences, and TOC rendering.

pages.title: Theme Runtime
pages.content:
## Templates

ZeroPress themes use `layout.html`, route templates, partials, and theme-owned assets.

## Render Context

Templates receive route data, menus, taxonomies, collections, and site metadata from the build step.

### Common Context

Common values include `site`, `menus`, `widgets`, `taxonomies`, and `collections`.

### Route Context

Page routes expose `page`; post routes expose `post`; taxonomy routes expose `taxonomy`.
pages.excerpt: A short overview of the ZeroPress theme runtime.

pages.title: HTML Content Example
pages.content:
Trusted HTML

HTML documents are rendered as provided

This page uses document_type: "html". ZeroPress treats this body as trusted content and places it inside the docs template.

Use this for migrated pages, embedded demos, or hand-authored static sections.

For general documentation, Markdown remains the preferred authoring format.
pages.excerpt: A small trusted HTML page rendered inside the docs template.
